claim: Always serialize timestamps in UTC to avoid cross-region comparison bugs
rationale: prior incidents with mixed local/UTC timestamps in order ledger

claim: Next.js route group folders like (protected) are filesystem-only and never appear in browser URLs. The isSellerRoute middleware matcher must use the actual URL paths (/seller/dashboard, /seller/edit, /seller/upload) — never the folder names.
rationale: proxy.ts had '/(protected)/seller(.*)' which never matched any real URL. /seller/edit and /seller/upload were silently unprotected — currentUser() returned null and triggered redirect('/') instead of loading the page. Pain score: 8.

claim: generateMetadata() in Next.js Server Components must never throw — wrap in try/catch and return a fallback. An uncaught throw inside generateMetadata surfaces as a cryptic unrelated error (e.g. ClerkAPIResponseError) at the page level.
rationale: EditProductPage crashed with 'ClerkAPIResponseError' because generateMetadata called getProductById which threw on Supabase errors. The real error was hidden. Fixed by wrapping in try/catch and making getProductById return null instead of throwing.

claim: Service methods (e.g. getProductById) that are called from both page components AND generateMetadata must return null on all error paths — never throw. Throwing from a service causes the entire Server Component tree to fail with misleading errors.
rationale: ProductService.getProductById threw on non-404 Supabase errors. This caused generateMetadata (which called it) to crash the page render with a fake ClerkAPIResponseError. Consistent return-null pattern prevents this category of bug.

claim: Browser <input type='file'> cannot be pre-populated for security reasons — it always shows 'No file chosen'. In edit forms with existing files, use a hidden sr-only input + a custom Button that triggers it via ref.current.click(), and show the current filename in a controlled span alongside it.
rationale: Seller edit form showed 'No file chosen' for both cover image and product file even though files already existed in Supabase. Fixed by replacing native file input UI with a custom button + controlled state span pattern.

claim: When new DB columns are added to existing tables (e.g. file_size, file_extension in Phase 2F), existing rows will have NULL. Always provide a SQL backfill script AND a display-layer fallback (e.g. derive file_extension from the file_url path). Don't rely on column data being present for pre-migration rows.
rationale: Phase 2F added file_size and file_extension columns. All existing products showed N/A on the product detail page. Fixed by (a) deriving extension from file_url path in the UI and (b) providing a SQL backfill for the extension column.

claim: Never divide price by 100 twice — if a variable already converts cents to dollars at declaration (e.g. const price = product.price / 100), do NOT divide again in the JSX display. Always trace the full data path from database (cents) to display (dollars) before touching price logic.
rationale: During Phase 3 Step 2, the agent changed the ProductCard bottom bar and introduced a double-division bug: price was divided by 100 at const price = product.price / 100, then displayed as (price / 100).toFixed(2), making all products show as less than a dollar. Victor caught this and was frustrated. Root cause: not tracing the existing price variable before modifying the JSX.

claim: **Next.js route groups are filesystem-only** — never appear in browser URLs. Middleware matchers must use real URL paths (e.g. `/seller/edit`) not folder names (e.g. `/(protected)/seller`). A wrong pattern silently skips auth, causing `currentUser()` to return null and triggering spurious redirects.
rationale: Imported from pre-restructure LESSONS.md bullets below sentinel

claim: **`generateMetadata()` must never throw** — wrap in try/catch and return a safe fallback title. An uncaught error inside `generateMetadata` surfaces as a cryptic unrelated error (e.g. ClerkAPIResponseError) at the page level, masking the true cause.
rationale: Imported from pre-restructure LESSONS.md bullets below sentinel

claim: **Service methods called from both page components and `generateMetadata` must return null on all error paths** — never throw. Throwing from a shared service crashes the entire Server Component tree with misleading errors.
rationale: Imported from pre-restructure LESSONS.md bullets below sentinel

claim: **`<input type="file">` cannot be pre-populated** (browser security). In edit forms with existing files, use a hidden `sr-only` input triggered by `ref.current.click()` from a custom Button, and display the current filename in a controlled `<span>` via state.
rationale: Imported from pre-restructure LESSONS.md bullets below sentinel

claim: **After adding columns to an existing table, always provide a SQL backfill AND a display-layer fallback** for pre-migration rows (which will be NULL). For string fields like `file_extension`, derive from the stored URL path as fallback.
rationale: Imported from pre-restructure LESSONS.md bullets below sentinel

claim: **Clerk `currentUser()` can return null if middleware didn't run auth for the route.** Never use `user?.id || ''` — this silently queries DB with an empty string. Always guard with `if (!user) redirect('/')` before any DB operation. Use a multi-field displayName fallback chain: `firstName || fullName || username || email.split('@')[0] || 'Seller'`.
rationale: Imported from pre-restructure LESSONS.md bullets below sentinel

claim: Never use 'any' type to silence TypeScript errors — always create proper type mappings. When a compatibility wrapper returns data for components expecting a specific type (e.g. Product[]), map every required field with sensible defaults instead of casting to any[].
rationale: During Zustand cart migration, the agent used 'as any[]' in hooks/useCart.ts to silence TS errors caused by CartItem not matching the full Product type. Victor explicitly flagged this as unacceptable because it defeats the entire purpose of strict TypeScript. The fix was to create a proper CartItem-to-Product mapping with default values for all required fields.

claim: When editing a file, never move code blocks outside their parent container accidentally — always verify the structural nesting of JSX before and after the edit. A conditional wrapper moved outside a flex container silently breaks layout alignment on different screen sizes.
rationale: Upload New Product button was moved outside the flex container during a conditional-visibility edit, breaking desktop right-alignment. Caught by Victor in landscape testing.

claim: When the user provides exact code snippets in their plan, implement them exactly as written — do not rewrite, optimize, or substitute equivalent expressions. Deviating from explicit user code introduces subtle bugs and erodes trust.
rationale: Victor provided exact price formatting code using (price / 100).toFixed(2) in JSX. Agent substituted with a pre-divided variable and toLocaleString, introducing a double-division bug that made all product prices appear under a dollar.

claim: Before editing any file, verify which lines are within your scope and which are not — never touch imports, variables, or logic blocks that are unrelated to the current task. Read the full file context first, identify the exact target lines, and leave everything else untouched.
rationale: Multiple incidents where agent accidentally removed or altered unrelated code during targeted edits. Victor explicitly warned: I asked u to stop making such mistakes. The root cause is rushing edits without verifying the full surrounding context.

claim: For Tempire 80% Shift layout, prefer lightweight UI patterns (Popover dropdowns, inline modals) over heavy full-screen overlays (Sheet, side drawers). A full-width Sheet feels too heavy on a centered max-w-7xl layout and blocks content unnecessarily.
rationale: Victor rejected the Sheet-based CartDrawer because it felt too heavy for the 80% Shift centered layout. Replaced with a Popover-based CartDropdown that hangs from the icon without blocking the page.

claim: When migrating state management (e.g. TanStack Query to Zustand), create a properly typed compatibility wrapper that maps every field of the old type with sensible defaults — never use 'as any' to bypass type errors. The wrapper must satisfy the full interface contract of consuming components.
rationale: During Zustand cart migration, agent initially cast items as any[] to silence TS errors. Victor caught this immediately and provided a proper CartItem-to-Product mapping with all 15+ required fields defaulted. This is now the established pattern for store migrations in Tempire.

claim: Always run npx tsc --noEmit after every code change and before reporting completion to the user. Never report success without a clean type check. This catches regressions early and maintains the strict TypeScript discipline Victor expects.
rationale: Established pattern across all Phase 2-3 work. Every step in the revised implementation plan ends with a tsc check. Victor expects zero type errors before manual testing.

claim: Tempire prices are stored in cents (integer) in Supabase. The single source of truth for conversion is: divide by 100 exactly once, at the display layer in JSX. Never create an intermediate variable that pre-divides and then divide again in the template string.
rationale: Double-division bug in ProductCard.tsx caused all prices to display at 1/100th of their actual value. Victor caught this in manual testing. The correct pattern is: const price = product.price (raw cents), then in JSX use (price / 100).toFixed(2).

claim: Victor works in small, testable steps — never implement more than one step at a time. Wait for manual test confirmation before proceeding to the next step. Each step should have clear affected files, exact code changes, and manual test steps listed upfront.
rationale: This is Victor's core development philosophy documented in AGENTS.md, CONSTITUTION.md and reinforced in every conversation. Steps 1-3 of Phase 3 each required separate approval cycles. Rushing ahead causes compounding errors.
